subject: Cloud Computing
title: Cloud and Startup Speed
description: A short lesson to help you apply Cloud and Startup Speed.

hook.text: In 2005, launching a tech startup required $1 million in servers. Today, it takes a credit card and $50.

buildup.text: Cloud eliminated the biggest startup barrier: upfront infrastructure cost. Ideas could go live in hours.

discovery.text: Instagram had 13 employees serving 30 million users — all running on AWS. That was impossible a decade earlier.

twist.text: The flip side: low barriers mean more competition. When anyone can launch, everyone does.

climax.text: Cloud democratized technology. The winners are now decided by ideas and execution, not capital budgets.

punchline.text: Cloud leveled the startup field. Now the best idea wins.

quiz.question: How did cloud computing change the startup landscape?
options: It eliminated upfront infrastructure costs, letting anyone launch quickly
It made startups more expensive to run than before
It required startups to build their own data centers first
